evals.prompt: Audit this codebase. Identify bugs, code quality issues, error handling gaps, security concerns, testing gaps, and any other concrete problems. Report only issues that can be fixed via pull request.
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report with prioritized findings, each including file paths, specific issue descriptions, and concrete fixes. No feature suggestions or user questions.
expectations: Every finding includes a specific file path (not just a module or area name)
Every finding includes a concrete fix description (not vague advice like 'improve error handling')
Findings are organized by severity levels (critical/high/medium/low or equivalent)
The report does not suggest adding new features
The report does not ask the user any questions or request clarification
The report includes a summary or overview section
Repeated instances of the same pattern are consolidated rather than listed individually
The known bug at subcommands.go where ModeMarathon is hardcoded is identified

evals.prompt: Scan this codebase for bugs, error handling issues, and security concerns. Report findings with file paths and specific fixes. Do not suggest new features.
evals.expected_output: A focused report on bugs, error handling, and security only. Each finding has a file path, concrete description, and specific fix. No feature suggestions, no architecture advice.
expectations: Every finding includes a specific file path (not just a module or area name)
Every finding includes a concrete fix description (not vague advice like 'improve error handling')
Findings are organized by severity levels (critical/high/medium/low or equivalent)
The report does not suggest adding new features
The report does not ask the user any questions or request clarification
The report stays focused on bugs, error handling, and security (does not expand scope to code style, documentation, etc.)
The known bug at subcommands.go where ModeMarathon is hardcoded is identified
The nil error wrapping bug at subcommands.go is identified

evals.prompt: Run a code health check on this project. Identify anything that's broken, dangerous, or degrading code quality. Every finding should be specific enough to generate a pull request from.
evals.expected_output: A health check report with PR-actionable findings. Each finding is specific enough that an automated system could write a PR from it. No vague advice or feature requests.
expectations: Every finding includes a specific file path (not just a module or area name)
Every finding includes a concrete fix description (not vague advice like 'improve error handling')
Findings are organized by severity levels (critical/high/medium/low or equivalent)
The report does not suggest adding new features
The report does not ask the user any questions or request clarification
Each finding is specific enough that a PR could be written from it without further clarification
The known bug at subcommands.go where ModeMarathon is hardcoded is identified
The database connection leak (no db.Close) is identified
